title: Translations. -Expectation And Fulfilment. (From Schiller.)
content:
In these epigrams I have altered the form, which in the original is the elegiac distich.



    Thousand-masted, mighty float,
     Out to sea Youth's navy goes:
    Silent, in his one saved boat,
     Age into the harbour rows.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, George MacDonald delivers a powerful performance in "Translations. -Expectation And Fulfilment. (From Schiller.)"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
